title: GrievanceHub Analysis Report
question: Management canceled previously approved annual leave without explanation and ignored the union's information request. What rules apply and what remedy is appropriate?
known_facts: Annual leave was approved in writing on May 12 for June 10–14.
Management canceled the leave on June 7 without citing an emergency.
The steward requested the cancellation notice and supporting records on June 8.
Management has not responded to the information request.

issue_analysis.primary_issue: Cancellation of previously approved annual leave

dispute_frame.action: canceled previously approved annual leave and did not respond to the union information request

case_information.case_title: Approved annual leave canceled without notice

report.research_draft_notice: This analysis is a research starting point, not legal advice. Review all citations and arguments before using it in a grievance.
report.your_question: Management canceled previously approved annual leave without explanation and ignored the union's information request. What rules apply and what remedy is appropriate?

quick_assessment.summary: The stated facts describe cancellation of pre-approved annual leave shortly before the scheduled absence and an unanswered union information request. Retrieved contract and manual authorities address approved-leave entitlement, emergency exceptions, information rights, and grievance remedies. If the facts are confirmed, the cancellation may implicate Article 10 leave protections and Article 17 information obligations.
quick_assessment.why: Assessment is grounded in retrieved Article 10 and Article 17 authorities and the stated facts only. Outcome depends on whether management can document a qualifying emergency and whether local LMOU provisions apply once indexed.

secondary_issues: Failure to respond to union information request

key_contract_violations.why_relevant: Contract language limits cancellation of approved annual leave to emergency situations.
key_contract_violations.direct_quote: Employees who have annual leave approved are entitled to such annual leave except in emergency situations.

key_contract_violations.why_relevant: Management must furnish information necessary for the union to process a grievance.
key_contract_violations.direct_quote: Upon request, the Employer shall furnish the Union with information necessary for the processing of a grievance.

union_supporting_authority.why_relevant: Defines the emergency exception management must meet to cancel approved leave.
union_supporting_authority.direct_quote: Emergency situations are limited to those that could not have been anticipated.

procedural_requirements.why_relevant: Grievance must be filed within contractual time limits from when the union learned of the violation.
procedural_requirements.direct_quote: A grievance must be filed within fourteen (14) days of when the grieving party first learned of the violation.

remedy_authority.why_relevant: Contract provides for corrective action and make-whole relief for proven violations.
remedy_authority.direct_quote: Where a violation is found, the Employer shall take corrective action and make the employee whole.

management_limiting_authority.why_relevant: Management may argue operational needs or unanticipated circumstances required schedule changes.
management_limiting_authority.direct_quote: The Employer retains the right to assign employees and to schedule work according to operational requirements.

supporting_evidence.what_it_supports: Written leave approval notice dated May 12 showing approved June 10–14 dates
supporting_evidence.how_to_use: Attach to grievance file to establish pre-approved leave.

supporting_evidence.what_it_supports: Management cancellation notice dated June 7 and any stated reason
supporting_evidence.how_to_use: Compare stated reason against the emergency exception standard.

supporting_evidence.what_it_supports: Written information request dated June 8 and proof of delivery
supporting_evidence.how_to_use: Document management's failure to respond under Article 17.3.

statements: Reinstate the previously approved annual leave for June 10–14 if no qualifying emergency is documented.
Provide all records relied upon for the cancellation, including any emergency determination.
Make the grievant whole for any leave or pay loss caused by the cancellation.
Respond promptly and completely to the union's information request.

detailed_analysis.grievance_framework: Whether management violated Article 10 by canceling previously approved annual leave without a qualifying emergency and whether management violated Article 17 by failing to respond to the union's information request regarding the cancellation.
evidence_to_gather: Complete written leave approval showing approved dates
Management cancellation notice and date of delivery
Any management claim of emergency and supporting records
Copy of the union information request and proof of delivery
Payroll or leave records showing any leave or pay loss
Witness statements regarding the cancellation conversation

strategic_tips.title: Document the timeline
strategic_tips.text: Build a clear chronology from written approval through cancellation and the unanswered information request. Verify grievance filing deadlines under Article 15.2 from the date the union learned of each violation.

strategic_tips.title: Information request follow-up
strategic_tips.text: Use Article 17.3 to request the cancellation notice, emergency determination, and any staffing records management relied upon. Document non-response in the grievance record.

strategic_tips.title: Emergency exception analysis
strategic_tips.text: Compare management's stated reason against the CIM emergency standard. If the situation could have been anticipated before June 7, management may not meet the emergency exception.

strategic_tips.title: Management-rights distinction
strategic_tips.text: Address Article 3.1 management-rights language separately. Operational scheduling authority does not automatically override approved-leave protections in Article 10.

report.matching_grievance_templates_notice: Grievance template matching is not yet available.

missing_facts: Whether management documented a qualifying emergency
Whether any local LMOU leave provisions apply
known_facts: Annual leave was approved in writing on May 12 for June 10–14.
Management canceled the leave on June 7 without citing an emergency.
The steward requested the cancellation notice and supporting records on June 8.
Management has not responded to the information request.
caveats: LMOU / local memorandum language was referenced but is not currently indexed in GrievanceHub.
This report is a research draft, not legal advice or a final grievance decision.
